dimensions.note: Workflow-scope report. It consumes @swamp/aws/s3/bucket and bucket-policy step data already persisted in swamp and makes no AWS calls of its own, so it handles no credentials or secrets.

info on run start and on the findings summary (buckets/fail/warn/skip/pass counts); warn when bucket or policy data is missing/unparseable and when the failOn gate trips (with a sample of trippers). Structured placeholders throughout; no secrets logged.

The report never throws: missing or schema-mismatched bucket/policy data becomes a skip-status finding (recordUnknown), and an attached-but-unparseable policy is distinguished from a no-policy bucket so policy rules skip honestly instead of false-passing. The failOn gate is advisory inside the report (surfaced in JSON + a warn log) rather than thrown, because swamp persists only a generic fallback artifact for a thrown report and the rich findings JSON would be lost.

117 Deno.test cases in tests/ covering each rule (versioning, encryption, public-access block, ownership, TLS-only policy condition parsing, lifecycle, logging, tags), the skip/unparseable paths, policy-document normalization edge cases, gate-tripper selection, and markdown/JSON rendering. NOTE: swamp's push gate emits a separate testing-completeness warning here because the suite lives only under tests/ with no test file colocated beside the entrypoint; that is a structural heuristic, not a coverage gap, and is out of scope for this review.

Pure read-and-render with no persisted resources and no external mutation; re-running over the same step data yields the same findings. Resilient to missing/corrupt upstream artifacts (skip findings) and to buckets seen only via a method-arg fallback identifier.

Makes no external HTTP/AWS calls. Its only contract is the @swamp/aws/s3 bucket and bucket-policy data shapes, which it re-validates with Zod safeParse (BucketStateSchema/BucketPolicyStateSchema) before applying rules.
Creates no cloud resources, network connections, file handles, or temp files. Reads stored bytes via context.dataRepository.getContent and returns markdown + JSON; nothing to clean up or track.

README examples and the rendered report use placeholder bucket names and workflow names; no real IPs, internal hostnames, account ids, or bucket ARNs tied to real infrastructure. The recent release reworded a comment to satisfy the package scorer, with no infra leakage.
